task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenSIS application using the provided administrator credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS login screen with 'admin' entered in the username field, or the main OpenSIS dashboard visible indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Subject Areas configuration page within the Course Catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS interface displaying the 'Subject Areas' or 'Course Catalog' setup page, typically accessed via the scheduling or school setup menus.

task_completion.description: Add 'Computer Science' as a new Subject Area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Computer Science' typed into the new Subject Area title/name input field, or a confirmation screen showing it being saved.

task_completion.description: Add 'Fine Arts' as a new Subject Area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Fine Arts' typed into the new Subject Area title/name input field, or a confirmation screen showing it being saved.

task_completion.description: Verify both subject areas are successfully saved in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list view or summary table in the OpenSIS Subject Areas page clearly displaying both 'Computer Science' and 'Fine Arts' as configured subjects.

integrity.description: The agent must use the web browser to interact with OpenSIS.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the OpenSIS graphical user interface. There should be no terminal windows executing curl requests or scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent must not use direct database manipulation to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL CLI commands (e.g., INSERT INTO course_subjects) or database management tools like phpMyAdmin.

integrity.description: The agent must genuinely enter the data into the application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The target strings ('Computer Science', 'Fine Arts') should be visible inside the standard OpenSIS input fields, not just edited into the DOM via browser developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring OpenSIS v9.2. The agent must log in using username 'admin' and password 'Admin@123'. The goal is to add two specific subject areas: 'Computer Science' and 'Fine Arts'. These are stored in the 'course_subjects' table but must be added via the OpenSIS web interface. Look for the agent navigating to the Course Catalog/Subject Areas menu and filling out the forms for these two subjects.
